title: credential lease batcher and typed stream-sink ergonomics (Go SDK)
preview: Context and motivation: Customers running long-lived streaming clients observed token churn when multiple concurrent streams re-auth at similar windows, and SDK ergonomics for stream sinks in Go lagged behind the typed async-iterable ergonomics in other SDKs. This PR implements a credential lease batcher to reduce r...
text: Context and motivation: Customers running long-lived streaming clients observed token churn when multiple concurrent streams re-auth at similar windows, and SDK ergonomics for stream sinks in Go lagged behind the typed async-iterable ergonomics in other SDKs. This PR implements a credential lease batcher to reduce reauth storming, introduces a typed StreamSink interface to improve ergonomics and type-safety, and adds an adaptive retry-weight profile specifically for streaming paths. Summary of changes and motivation: 1) credential/lease: new reauth.BatchScheduler and lease.Pool interfaces (concrete implementation BatchLeaser) that coalesce near-simultaneous credential refreshes into a single token fetch per host/region window, lowering QPS to auth backends. 2) streaming: add typed stream Sink and SinkAdapter to replace less-idiomatic callback sinks while providing a compatibility shim...

title: Streaming: typed disconnect errors w/ request_id + docs on safe reconnect/retry patterns
preview: Context: This PR is part of the cross-language SDK parity work for streaming semantics and error taxonomy. We’ve had multiple reports (SUP-29505) of streaming disconnects behind proxies/load balancers where the Go SDK returned opaque io.EOF / net errors without request_id, making Support/oncall triage slow (INT-1182...
text: Context: This PR is part of the cross-language SDK parity work for streaming semantics and error taxonomy. We’ve had multiple reports (SUP-29505) of streaming disconnects behind proxies/load balancers where the Go SDK returned opaque io.EOF / net errors without request_id, making Support/oncall triage slow (INT-1182). Also, customers asked for clearer guidance on whether they should retry streaming calls, and under what conditions (avoid duplicated completions / side effects). What this PR does: (1) Introduces typed streaming error surfaces so callers can distinguish server-side HTTP failures (pre-stream) vs transport disconnects (mid-stream) vs user cancellation/timeouts. (2) Propagates request_id and status_code onto those error types whenever we can infer them from headers/initial response. (3) Adds docs + examples for safe retry patterns and reconnect guidance, with explicit idemp...

title: Align timeout model + ensure context cancellation aborts HTTP and streaming reads
preview:
Context
We’re standardizing timeout behavior across Python/TS/Go. The Go SDK currently mixes (a) http.Client.Timeout, (b) ad-hoc context.WithTimeout usage, and (c) streaming loops that can keep blocking reads even after ctx cancellation. The result is inconsistent behavior across request types and, in a few cases, g...
text:
Context
We’re standardizing timeout behavior across Python/TS/Go. The Go SDK currently mixes (a) http.Client.Timeout, (b) ad-hoc context.WithTimeout usage, and (c) streaming loops that can keep blocking reads even after ctx cancellation. The result is inconsistent behavior across request types and, in a few cases, goroutine leaks + hung streaming reads when callers cancel.

This PR aligns the Go SDK with the timeout ADR direction (overall + granular connect/read) and makes ctx cancellation reliably abort:
- non-streaming requests (request/response)
- streaming requests (SSE/event-stream) including when blocked in io.Read / bufio reads

What changed
1) New unified timeout configuration
- Introduce sdk.Timeouts:
 - Overall: total wall-clock time for the request (0 = no overall timeout)
 - Connect: max time to establish connection + receive response headers
 - Read: max idle time waiting...

text:
## Summary
This PR implements the SDK parity retry standard for the Go SDK: retryable status codes + network errors, exponential backoff with jitter, and Retry-After parsing/precedence. It also adds a public configuration surface so users can tune or disable retries consistently across languages.

Motivation:
- Fixes customer-reported behavior where the Go SDK ignored Retry-After and retried too aggressively during rate limiting (SUP-29477).
- Aligns with the cross-language standard for predictable behavior and easier support/debugging.

## What changed
### Defaults (v1)
- **Retryable HTTP status codes:** 408, 409, 429, 500, 502, 503, 504
- **Retryable network errors:** connection reset, EOF mid-response before headers, temporary errors, and timeouts from the underlying net/http stack
- **Not retried:** context cancellation/deadline exceeded initiated by the caller, and any 4xx other ...

title: Align auth env vars + Authorization header formatting; redact secrets in debug logs
sdk(go): add streaming reconnect helper + example for resilient chat streams
preview: Context: We’ve seen a recurring onboarding/first-prod issue where customers try streaming in a quickstart-like environment and interpret transient disconnects (mobile networks, reverse proxies, idle timeouts) as SDK instability. Support ticket SUP-18488 captures a representative failure mode. This PR adds a small, o...
text:
Context: We’ve seen a recurring onboarding/first-prod issue where customers try streaming in a quickstart-like environment and interpret transient disconnects (mobile networks, reverse proxies, idle timeouts) as SDK instability. Support ticket SUP-18488 captures a representative failure mode. This PR adds a small, opinionated helper for resilient streaming consumption in the Go SDK and a runnable example to show recommended patterns.

What’s included: (1) A new streaming reconnect helper in package streamx that wraps our existing streaming client and provides controlled reconnect behavior. It retries on network/transport errors and selected HTTP statuses (408/429/5xx) with exponential backoff + jitter, respects Retry-After, and uses a bounded max elapsed time / max attempts. (2) A lightweight event cursor that lets callers preserve progress (last received event ID / last chunk index) ...

title: Tolerate trailing newline in API key and improve auth error messaging
preview: Context: SUP-4902. Customers reading API keys from mounted files often include a trailing newline. The Go SDK previously validated the API key string strictly and returned a generic 'invalid api key' error, causing confusion. Changes: if key ends with CR/LF, trim only those characters; if other whitespace exists, re...
text: Context: SUP-4902. Customers reading API keys from mounted files often include a trailing newline. The Go SDK previously validated the API key string strictly and returned a generic 'invalid api key' error, causing confusion. Changes: if key ends with CR/LF, trim only those characters; if other whitespace exists, return a clearer error message pointing to the whitespace; add unit tests; update README guidance for Kubernetes secret mounts. Release: v0.6.2.

title: Introduce smart credential window and compact stream facade (Go SDK v1.4.1)
preview: Motivation: Multiple customer incidents and internal benchmarks showed that short-lived credential refresh windows and ad-hoc retry knobs led to transient auth failures in long-lived streaming sessions. In parallel, Go SDK consumers requested a smaller streaming facade that supports pause/resume, context-aware cance...
text:
Motivation: Multiple customer incidents and internal benchmarks showed that short-lived credential refresh windows and ad-hoc retry knobs led to transient auth failures in long-lived streaming sessions. In parallel, Go SDK consumers requested a smaller streaming facade that supports pause/resume, context-aware cancellation, and better default backoff behavior so they don't reimplement session glue. This PR implements a 'SmartCredentialWindow' alongside a compact 'StreamFacade' and tightens default retry ergonomics for streaming APIs.

What this PR does (single-line sections kept as a contiguous description): Introduces sdk/go/creds/window.go implementing SmartCredentialWindow which coalesces refresh attempts, exposes a small lifecycle API (StartLease, ExtendIfNeeded, Remaining) and integrates with the existing SessionManager; adds sdk/go/stream/facade.go - a compact streaming facade w...
